title: [machamp] Battle Guide
summary: A [fighting] physical tank whose [no-guard] turns [dynamic-punch] into a 100% accurate guaranteed-confuse move — the damage anchor of a trick-room team.
strengths: 130 Attack with bulky EVs breaks walls
weaknesses: Speed 55 loses to almost everything unboosted
Flying, psychic, and Fairy weaknesses are common

recommendedMoves.reason: Dark utility to punish Ghost and Psychic switch-ins
Rock coverage for Flying and Rock walls
Priority cleanup for the Speed problem

recommendedItems.reason: Long-game healing for the bulky set

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[machamp] is a [fighting]-type physical tank whose identity revolves around the hidden ability [no-guard]. The ability forces every move to hit, which is a liability in theory but a one-card advantage with [dynamic-punch]: the move has 50 accuracy and 100% confusion chance, so [no-guard] turns it into a guaranteed 100 BP STAB that also confuses every target. Combined with 130 Attack and 90/80/85 bulk, [machamp] is the damage anchor of trick-room cores.

## Strengths
[no-guard] + [dynamic-punch] is unique — a 100 BP [fighting] STAB that always hits and always confuses the target is a win condition by itself. 130 Attack lets even bulky EV spreads push through walls, and [bullet-punch] provides a priority slot that patches the 55 Speed problem. [knock-off] adds item removal and Ghost coverage, and [stone-edge] gains 100% accuracy from [no-guard] for clean Rock coverage.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Speed 55 means [machamp] moves last against almost everything outside trick-room. Flying, psychic, and Fairy attackers all threaten KOs before it can punch, and Ghost types are immune to [dynamic-punch] entirely — [knock-off] and [stone-edge] are the only answers to those switch-ins. The set also struggles against priority moves that punish the slow speed tier.

## Recommended Moveset
[dynamic-punch] is locked as the main STAB because it is the whole reason the set exists. [knock-off] handles Ghost and Psychic switch-ins while also stripping items for long-game value. [stone-edge] is the Rock coverage that benefits from [no-guard] accuracy fixing, and [bullet-punch] is the last slot as a priority cleaner for fast threats. A heavy-slam variant exists for dedicated Fairy answers.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default for the bulky tank role. [life-orb] is the offensive alternative that trades longevity for damage. [focus-sash] insures the first setup turn on non-trick-room teams. Ability must be [no-guard] — guts and steadfast are not competitive. Tera [fighting] maxes [dynamic-punch] damage, and Tera [steel] flips Flying, Fairy, and psychic weaknesses into resistances.

## Teammates & Synergy
[machamp] is the trick-room win condition, so a dedicated trick-room setter is almost mandatory. A Dark or [steel] partner covers Psychic and Fairy weaknesses, and item-removal support stacks with [knock-off] to drain the opposing resource pool. A cleaner behind [machamp] converts the confuse turns into guaranteed KOs.

## Play Patterns
Set trick-room on turn one, switch [machamp] in on turn two, then [dynamic-punch] anything that is not a Ghost. Swap to [knock-off] or [stone-edge] on Ghost or Flying switch-ins, and use [bullet-punch] to finish weakened faster attackers. Tera on any telegraphed psychic or Fairy move.
